title: Slow-cooker pot roast
subtitle: Whole braising joint with root vegetables and beef stock
excerpt: A large piece of beef chuck or brisket braised whole with root vegetables, herbs, and beef stock until it collapses. No carving. It pulls apart with two spoons at the table. The cooking juices become the gravy. One of the slow cooker's most capable dishes.
sourceNotes: Pot roast is an American term for a braised beef joint, itself descended from the French pot-au-feu and the British boiled beef tradition. The slow cooker version became a standard American household recipe in the 1970s and subsequently spread to British kitchens. The principle is unchanged: a whole piece of braising beef cooked in its own juices with vegetables until completely tender.

content.text: The joint must sit above the liquid, not submerged in it. Place the vegetables on the bottom of the pot to form a trivet, then set the beef on top. It braises in the steam and the juices that rise rather than stewing in the stock, this is the key difference from a stew and what keeps the texture distinct.

items.name: Beef chuck
items.prepNote: as a whole piece, not diced

content.text: Season the {{beef-chuck}} all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Arrange the {{carrot}}, {{onion}}, {{celery}}, and {{garlic}} across the bottom of the slow cooker to form a trivet. Set the beef on top. Stir the {{tomato-puree}} and {{worcestershire-sauce}} into the {{stock-beef}} and pour around the beef, not over it. Lay the {{thyme-fresh}} and {{bay-leaves}} on top. Cook on low for 8 to 9 hours, until the beef yields when pressed and pulls apart easily. Transfer the beef to a board and rest for 15 minutes. Strain the cooking juices into a small saucepan. Simmer to reduce by half for a loose gravy or whisk in a little cornflour slurry to thicken.

items.fix: The vegetables as a trivet keep the beef above the braising liquid. If submerged it stews and can dry out. Check the lid seals fully
items.cause: Beef sitting in liquid rather than above it, or lid not sealing
items.symptom: Joint dry despite the long cook

items.fix: Keep vegetables in large chunks. They will soften completely but should still hold their shape enough to eat alongside the beef
items.cause: Cut too small. They overcook in 8 hours at small sizes
items.symptom: Vegetables turned to a grey mush

items.fix: Sear the beef in a very hot pan on all sides for 3 to 4 minutes total before placing in the slow cooker. The colour transfers to the braising liquid

content.text: Pot roast is a Sunday dinner or a weeknight meal that needs no attention after the first 15 minutes. The slow cooker produces a result that would require 3 hours of oven time to match. It is the kind of dish that makes the house smell right when the family comes in: beef, herbs, and the warmth of long cooking.
